---
title: "Étale cohomology of curves"
date: "2025-04-09"
description: >
   We study the cohomology of algebraic curves.
categories: [Advanced, Algebraic Geometry]
draft: true
---


::: {#lem-vanishcohomology}

Let $X$ be a finite scheme over a separably closed field $k$. Then $\mathrm{H}^i(X,\mathcal{F})=0$ for $i>0$ and for all $\mathcal{F}\in X_{ét}$.
:::

::: {.proof}

in progress...
:::


::: {#lem-support}

Let $X$ be a scheme, $i: Z\to X$ a closed immersion, and $\mathcal{F}\in X_{ét}$ supported on $Z$. Then the canonical morphism $\mathcal{F}\to i_*i^* \mathcal{F}$ is an isomorphism.
:::
::: {.proof}

in progress...
:::



::: {#cor-sumskyscraper}

## of @lem-support

Let $X$ be a scheme and $\mathcal{F}\in X_{ét}$ supported on finitely many closed points of $X$. Then 
$$
	\mathcal{F}\cong\bigoplus_{x\in \mathrm{supp}(\mathcal{F})}x_*x^* \mathcal{F}
$$
:::

::: {.proof}

in progress...
:::

::: {#lem-vanishingskyscraper}

Let $X$ be a scheme and $\mathcal{F}\in X_{ét}$ skyscrapersheaf $f:x\to X$ (riscriverò meglio). Then
$$
	\mathrm{R}^if_* \mathcal{F}=0
$$

In particular,
$$
	\mathrm{H}^i(X, \mathcal{F})=0
$$
for all $i>0$.
:::

::: {.proof}

in progress...
:::



::: {#lem-torsion}

Let $X$ be a quasi-compact quasi-separated (qcqs) scheme, and $\mathcal{F}\in X_{ét}$ be a torsion sheaf. Then $\mathrm{H}^i(X, \mathcal{F})$ is a torsion group for all $i\geq 0$.
:::

::: {.proof}

in progress...
:::


::: {#thm-multiplicative-sheaf}

Let $C$ be an algebraic curve over a separably closed field $k$. Then
$$
	\mathrm{H}^i(C, \mathcal{O}_{C,ét})=
	\begin{cases}
		\mathcal{O}_C(C)^\times &i=0\\
		\mathrm{Pic}(C) &i=1 \\
		0 &i>1 \text{ and $k$ is algebraically closed}\\
		p^\infty \text{ torsion group } &i>1 \text{ and $k$ is non perfect with char($k$)=p}
	\end{cases}
$$

:::

::: {.proof}

in progress...
:::
